title: 'The Batman' Is Here!
format: Sean, Joanna, and Chris review Matt Reeves's nearly three-hour, year-two Batman reboot, starring Robert Pattinson as a grungier, more traumatized Bruce Wayne opposite Zoe Kravitz, Paul Dano, and Colin Farrell. Then Sean sits down with Reeves to talk about noir influences and practical stunts, and asks why he wanted an early-days detective story instead of another origin tale.

segments.heading: 'The Batman'
segments.note: the bat and the cat weigh in

films.title: The Batman
films.note: Matt Reeves's year-two reboot, heavily indebted to Frank Miller's 'Year One' and Darwyn Cooke's Ego run, with Robert Pattinson as a more insomniac, emotionally raw Batman opposite Zoe Kravitz's Selina Kyle, Paul Dano's Riddler, Jeffrey Wright, Colin Farrell, John Turturro, Peter Sarsgaard, and Andy Serkis. Joanna loved it start to finish; Chris admired it more than he enjoyed it, calling the nearly three-hour runtime 'a slog' at times and the PG-13 rating a constraint on how dark Reeves could go.

films.title: Batman: Mask of the Phantasm
films.note: Chris's self-described favorite movie of all time (he owns it on laserdisc); Sean cites it as a heavy influence on the new film's noir tone.

films.title: Batman
films.note: Tim Burton's original, and Chris's soft spot since it was the first Batman he saw on screen.

films.title: Batman Returns
films.note: Michelle Pfeiffer's Catwoman comes up as the group's touchstone for how a cat burglar has been played before; Joanna notes Kravitz's version is far more grounded and doesn't do anything 'catlike' beyond drinking milk.

films.title: The Dark Knight
films.note: Sean compares the Riddler's relationship with Batman to Ledger's Joker and Bale's Batman needing each other. Chris argues this new film shares the most tonal DNA with Nolan's trilogy, though Joanna finds Gotham here more visually distinct thanks to Greg Fraser's photography.

films.title: The Dark Knight Rises
films.note: Chris hears Occupy-era overtones in how the Riddler organizes his followers, comparable to Bane's rhetoric here; also the other reference point (with Hathaway) for a Catwoman running her own agenda alongside Batman's.

films.title: The Batman
films.note: Reeves describes wanting an 'early years' Batman who hasn't figured himself out, driven by trauma rather than heroism, opening on the line 'I am vengeance' instead of 'I'm Batman.' He talks through the Hitchcockian, subjective camera approach and the deliberately imperfect anamorphic lenses. The Batmobile chase was shot practically, with a hard-mounted camera, modeled on 'The French Connection' and 'Bullitt'; the finished digital image was then shot out to film and back to rough up the texture. The script dates to 2017; the pandemic stretched production to five years.

films.title: Cloverfield
films.note: Reeves recalls the viral 'Transformers' trailer moment creating outsized expectations before the movie was even finished, a feeling he says returned with 'The Batman.'

films.title: Let Me In
films.note: He calls it his most personal film going in, tied to being bullied as a kid; he wrote to original novelist John Ajvide Lindqvist, who urged him to make it.

films.title: Rise of the Planet of the Apes
films.note: Rupert Wyatt's franchise-starter; Reeves came aboard for the next two entries.

films.title: Dawn of the Planet of the Apes
films.note: Reeves's first Apes film, and the start of his ongoing collaboration with production designer James Chinlund, who also designed 'The Batman.'

films.title: War for the Planet of the Apes
films.note: Reeves was finishing this when he signed on to 'The Batman' in 2017; the two productions overlapped by roughly five years.

TV: The North Water (miniseries, referred to as 'the Northwater')
Ads: Netflix FIFA World Cup Launch Edition
